method: Word->numeral adjacency order in the token stream; within-inscription stream-shuffle null (FROZEN primary). PC = detect planted order + false-positive control on randomized order.
result: Raw pattern overwhelmingly word-first (word then quantity): p_wordfirst=0.982 (1040 word->num vs 19 num->word), consistent across ALL 8 testable sites (0.95-1.00). BUT the frozen stream-shuffle null FAILED its false-positive control (fp rate 0.48; orchestrator-confirmed anti-conservative), so no certified claim under this null.
verdict_null_model: stream_shuffle (frozen primary null) — PC false-positive rate 0.48 > 0.10, anti-conservative
key_findings: Word->numeral is the overwhelmingly dominant stream order: 1040 word->num vs 19 num->word (p_wordfirst=0.982) — the administrative 'entry then quantity' ledger pattern.
The direction is CONSISTENT across all 8 testable sites (per-site p_wordfirst 0.95-1.00), i.e. cross-site robust at the raw level.

The FROZEN primary null (within-inscription full stream-shuffle) FAILED its positive control: false-positive rate 0.48 (>>0.10) on order-randomized data — anti-conservative, orchestrator-confirmed (>=0.20).
Therefore no CERTIFIED significance claim under the frozen null; verdict MACHINERY_UNINFORMATIVE (fail-closed).
The raw signal is so strong + cross-site-consistent that a CALIBRATED null (pair-flip on word/num adjacencies only) is expected to certify it — queued as the E031 successor.
successor_hypotheses: E031: re-test word->numeral order with a PAIR-FLIP null (for each adjacent word/num pair, flip order with p=0.5) that is calibrated by construction — certify the ledger grammar properly.
Test whether the rare num->word cases (19) cluster (specific signs/sites/document classes) — a structural exception class.
Does word->numeral hold WITHIN document class (tablet vs roundel) controlling for E028 length-register?
Numeral MAGNITUDE vs word length/position (structural, no arithmetic on values).
Multi-numeral runs after a single word (list entries) vs one-numeral-per-word — ledger entry cardinality.
Fraction-sign vs integer-num ordering relative to words (is the fraction system positioned differently?).
non_circular: Token TYPES (word/num) and stream order are directly observed; no phonetic value, no arithmetic on numeral values, LB not used here; anonymous.
deviations: GLM's result.json was written missing key_findings + successor_hypotheses after 2 auto-repair passes; the ORCHESTRATOR completed result.json from GLM's verified _full_results.json (Art. XVII record-completion, analogous to E023's report). All numbers are GLM's, orchestrator-verified.

Orchestrator repro_check had a KEY BUG (used token type 'numeral'; the corpus uses 'num') which contributed a spurious NO_WORD_NUMERAL_ADJ gate problem; corrected in verification (word->num=1040 confirmed with the right key).
FROZEN primary null = stream_shuffle; it failed its PC (fp 0.48). A pair-flip null was noted as the calibrated successor (E031).
